{"society_power": 8.5, "state_power": 7.5, "society_change": 1.0, "state_change": 0.0, "key_event": "2011 student movement and durable protest ecosystem", "reasoning": "The 2011 \"Chilean Winter\" matured into a dense, durable ecosystem of student, feminist, Mapuche, and anti-AFP movements that structurally strengthened civil society and forced reforms onto the agenda, pushing society power to strong-dominant levels. State capacity remained robust and democratically bounded (alternation, electoral reform, tax/education reforms), but corruption scandals, post-boom fiscal constraints, and eroding elite legitimacy offset its expanded social ambitions, leaving state power roughly flat."}
